content.sentence: Chúng tôi sẽ mời ___ mọi người.

translation.en: We will invite all the people.

notes_md:
**tất cả** means *all* or *everything / everyone*, depending on what follows it. It talks about the whole group, not just part of it.

It usually comes before a noun or pronoun to mean 100% of something. Very often you’ll see **đều** after it to emphasize that every member does something.


Close synonym: **mọi** (as in “mọi người”), but *mọi* goes directly before the noun and doesn’t need “tất cả.” More formal synonym: **hết thảy**.

translation.en: We will invite all the people.

content.sentence: Chúng tôi sẽ mời ___ mọi người.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Anh has prepared all documents for the meeting this morning.
Chị wants to thank all people who helped her last week.
Em can eat all this food if em wants.

example.en: All the students arrived to class on time.
example.vi: Tất cả học sinh đều đã đến lớp đúng giờ.

definitions.en: Used to refer to the whole or every part of a group.
definitions.vi: Dùng để chỉ toàn bộ hoặc mọi thành phần trong một nhóm.

notes:
tất cả means all or everything / everyone, depending on what follows it. It talks about the whole group, not just part of it.

It usually comes before a noun or pronoun to mean 100% of something. Very often you’ll see đều after it to emphasize that every member does something.

Close synonym: mọi (as in “mọi người”), but mọi goes directly before the noun and doesn’t need “tất cả.” More formal synonym: hết thảy.
exampleSentences: Anh đã chuẩn bị tất cả tài liệu cho buổi họp sáng nay.
Chị muốn cảm ơn tất cả mọi người đã giúp đỡ chị tuần trước.
Em có thể ăn tất cả món ăn này nếu em muốn.
